name: Ubiquiti airMAX PrismStation Horn 60°
title: Ubiquiti airMAX PrismStation Horn 60°
title_extended: Ubiquiti airMAX PrismStation Horn 60° - 5GHz, 16 dBi, Beam Width 60°
short_description: 5GHz, 16 dBi, Beam Width 60°, pair with PrismStation AC or IsoStation
description: 5GHz, 16 dBi, Beam Width 60°, pair with PrismStation AC or IsoStation

detailed_description.heading: 5 GHz Beamwidth Horn antenna
detailed_description.text: 5 GHz Beamwidth Horn antenna with 60° precise radiation angles for specific beam shaping.

detailed_description.heading: Advanced Directional Horn Antenna
detailed_description.text: The airMAX PrismStation Horn 5 60° is an advanced directional horn antenna designed by Ubiquiti Networks, tailored to enhance and extend the capabilities of the airMAX PrismStation series. This device stands out in the wireless networking industry for its precision in beam shaping and exceptional interference mitigation, specifically engineered for both point-to-point (PtP) and point-to-multipoint (PtMP) configurations within the 5 GHz frequency spectrum. The Horn-5-60 is crafted to provide a perfect balance between wide coverage and focused signal strength, making it an essential component for network operators seeking to optimize their wireless infrastructure for high efficiency and reliability.

detailed_description.heading: Compatibility
detailed_description.text: Compatible with airFiber, LTU and airMAX radios.

detailed_description.heading: Twist-Lock Mechanism
detailed_description.text: Integration with the PrismStation series is straightforward, thanks to the antenna's twist-lock mechanism, which ensures a secure and simple connection without the need for specialized tools or extensive installation procedures. This design not only facilitates rapid deployment and system scalability but also allows network operators to easily swap or upgrade antenna components as network requirements evolve. Moreover, the Horn-5-60 is also designed with compatibility in mind, seamlessly working with other Ubiquiti airMAX or LTU devices that support the same frequency band and connection interface. This interoperability allows for the creation of highly customized network deployments, catering to the specific coverage and performance needs of each installation.

detailed_description.heading: 60° Beamwidth
detailed_description.text: Central to its design is a 60° beamwidth antenna that offers a unique combination of wide-area coverage and targeted signal concentration. This attribute allows the antenna to effectively distribute wireless signals across a broad area while maintaining the intensity and integrity of the signal, significantly enhancing network performance in diverse environments. The wide beamwidth is particularly beneficial in PtMP applications, where it can serve multiple client devices without sacrificing signal quality, thereby optimizing the use of the wireless spectrum and reducing the need for additional hardware. Also, 60° and 90° versions have naturally attenuated side lobes and extremely low back radiation. They offer the best front-to-back ratio in the industry and the lowest side lobe radiation. Asymmetrical horn antennas are ideal for cluster sector installations with high co-location requirements.

detailed_description.heading: RF Isolation Capabilities
detailed_description.text: A distinguishing feature of the Horn-5-60 is its unparalleled RF isolation capabilities. The horn antenna design inherently minimizes the reception of off-axis signals, which are a common source of interference and noise in crowded RF environments. This reduction in signal interference is critical for maintaining a clean and clear communication channel, ensuring high-quality wireless connectivity even in areas with high levels of RF noise. Such performance is indispensable for deploying robust wireless networks in urban settings, industrial sites, and other locations where multiple wireless devices operate simultaneously.

detailed_description.heading: Weather-Resistant Construction
detailed_description.text: Built to withstand the challenges of outdoor deployment, the Horn-5-60 features a rugged, weather-resistant construction. Its materials are selected for durability and resilience, ensuring that the antenna continues to perform optimally even in adverse weather conditions, including heavy rain, high winds, and extreme temperature variations. Such robustness is vital for maintaining the reliability and longevity of outdoor wireless networks, where equipment must endure constant exposure to the elements.
